channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-20100] writing of many small files below full BRW RPC size results in very bad write speed
item.description:
writing of many small files below full BRW RPC size results in very bad write speed:
 

When writing sequential files to the system we have noticed a drop in performance when writes files that are smaller than max pages per rpc size. For example running this

for x in {0001..5000}; do echo $x; dd bs=1M count=5 if=/dev/zero of=file$x; done

performance will be ~1G till the 143-145th file, then drop off only to recover later.
On the other hand running this

for x in {0001..5000}; do echo $x; dd bs=1M count=16 if=/dev/zero of=file$x; done

will result in no performance drop.
max_pages_per_rpc=4096
page size 4k

performance drop means "singe digit MB/s" instead of > 1GB/s, i.e. the slowdown is ~ 100x.
item.summary: writing of many small files below full BRW RPC size results in very bad write speed

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
Hello,

The amount of dirty memory in the GOOD tests never exceeds 10GB:

zam@Rogue lustre-wc-rel % zless ~/Downloads/ANL_Lustre_Tests_upstream_2.15.7/GOOD_flare_4M_files_20_vm_dirty_ratio_test.out | grep -i Dirty:
Dirty:                12 kB
Dirty:            203652 kB
Dirty:            406340 kB
Dirty:            610996 kB
Dirty:            816468 kB
Dirty:           1021060 kB
Dirty:           1226092 kB
Dirty:           1433220 kB
Dirty:           1637700 kB
Dirty:           1842500 kB
Dirty:           2015284 kB
Dirty:           2162524 kB
Dirty:           2334084 kB
Dirty:           2391860 kB
Dirty:           2519340 kB
Dirty:           2593964 kB
Dirty:           2635708 kB
...
Dirty:           9237552 kB
Dirty:           9441520 kB
Dirty:           9647288 kB
Dirty:           9851448 kB
Dirty:          10056432 kB
Dirty:          10224048 kB
Dirty:          10117328 kB
Dirty:          10227552 kB
Dirty:          10057304 kB
Dirty:          10211544 kB
Dirty:          10127864 kB
Dirty:          10192448 kB
Dirty:          10195584 kB
Dirty:          10177768 kB
Dirty:          10230296 kB
Dirty:          10126672 kB
Dirty:          10333008 kB
Dirty:          10073184 kB
Dirty:          10277984 kB
Dirty:           9728664 kB
Dirty:           8663600 kB
Dirty:           7396776 kB
Dirty:           6188040 kB
Dirty:           4901800 kB
...

So the it never hits the dirty limit set by vm.dirty_ratio=20. The throttling in the balance_dirty_pages() never works.

The new clients (including cray-2.15.B24) can dirty more memory, the logs show numbers up to 16GB and 17GB:

$ zless ~/Downloads/ANL_Lustre_Tests_1/flare_4M_files_20_vm_dirty_ratio_test.out | grep -i Dirty:
...
Dirty:          13344040 kB
Dirty:          13548392 kB
Dirty:          13753440 kB
Dirty:          13958504 kB
Dirty:          14162472 kB
Dirty:          14367080 kB
Dirty:          14573352 kB
Dirty:          14777768 kB
Dirty:          14982056 kB
Dirty:          15187952 kB
Dirty:          15391152 kB
Dirty:          15597488 kB
Dirty:          15802320 kB
Dirty:          16006720 kB
Dirty:          16211824 kB
Dirty:          16415536 kB
Dirty:          16621248 kB
...

triggering the throttling inside the balance_dirty_pages() with subsequent effects of long pauses during dd write and visible slowdown.

I do not know what actually limits the amount of dirty memory for lustre-2.15.7 client, but seems that limit doesn't work in lustre-2.16 or newer Lustre.

I suggested to change the machine-wide dirty mem limit to some value below 20% of the RAM, i.e. 5GB, 10GB and some bigger numbers should work.

lctl set_param max_dirty_mb=5000

The parameter is only exported in /sysfs . If it was a module parameter it would be easier to set it per-client, but it is not yet implemeted.

Thanks,
Zam.

OK, these are perfectly fine parameters - could likely be one patch - but have we investigated the root of the problem?  These tunings have various reasons to be set to other values.  It's just weird to have a "hey we have this performance problem" LU description and rather than "here's why", it's "let's add module parameters".

It seems like there's some more info, could we get that?

... setting the max_dirty_mb parameter to some threshold
below the one calculated from vm.dirty_ratio helped to recover the write speed.

the minimum fix would be in exporting obd_max_dirty_mb as a module parameter,
but also interesting to know why BDP throttling kills the write speed so badly
comparing to the native Lustre limit on amount of dirty mb .

Thanks,
Zam.

Some module parameters to ease setting of lustre cache / dirty page values to other than automatic defaults.

"Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65179
Subject: LU-20100 obdclass: obdclass: max_dirty_mb module parameter
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 95943411875347838f292948e3ddccfdb4a27217

"Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65178
Subject: LU-20100 obdclass: module parameter osc_max_dirty_mb_default
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: e36446aa4b8f53a896ef814cab281d43250cb258

"Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65177
Subject: LU-20100 obdclass: max_cached_mb module parameter
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 9c18f47b70a3655f8b6f353f78bd9012a25f22e5
The title and the description do not seem obviously connected, can you explain?
